finding: id=A1 scope=in_scope action=follow_up status=open severity=medium fix_role=none rationale=n2-R1 CONFIRMED-REAL-but-FENCED-at-verdict-time: a live kind:'gate' is invisible to the R1 else-branch and to liveReadsAtMerge (adaptive-node.js:5393) — demonstrated end-to-end on an in-grammar frozen plan ({main-session-gate ∥ doc-updater docs/api.md} antichain: gate opens via open-next, open-ready co-opens the writer as a size-1 leg group behind the live gate, close-node MERGES test-consumed prose into the parent while the gate is in_progress; main holds write_awaits_drain at the same state — the window is NEW in #641). No verdict-time violation was constructible: the merged bytes always belong to a node closed atomically in the same close-node call; a misbehaving writer (out-of-lane leg file) refused write_set_overflow BEFORE any parent mutation; G3 is write-set-aware (production-set ∥ gate refuses at freeze) so only docs-band bytes can ever land; orient at the gate-live state returns frontier:[] / enterBatch:false / inProgressNode:gate and the resume card routes re-dispatch/commit-bracket (never open-ready); the reverse route (gate behind live reads) refuses scheduler_active. Residual: torn observation of CLOSED docs-band work (incl. docs/api.md) only if an orchestrator interleaves close-node inside its own inline gate check via out-of-card background instrumentation. Follow-up: count kind:'gate' at liveReadsAtMerge + hold the relaxed else-branch and tryR2bLeglessCoopen while a kind:'gate' member is live.
finding: id=A2 scope=in_scope action=follow_up status=open severity=low fix_role=none rationale=n2-R2 CONFIRMED-REAL: testConsumedExtra is not threaded into tryR2bLeglessCoopen's scratchObservableWriteSet call — runtime demo: a fork plan declaring validation_test_consumes: docs/fork-guide.md still R2b-co-opens a docs/fork-guide.md writer behind the scratch gate over a dirty parent, though the predicate itself refuses that path when the extra IS passed. Bounded: fires only on a self-hosting fork with the widening, and any verdict impact requires the annotated gate to disobey its pinned scratch-only contract (run the fork's chains mid-edit); no post-domination ⇒ no laundering channel. One-arg thread-through (parseValidationTestConsumes → opts.testConsumedExtra) when next touched.

## Claim Under Test
"The #641 relaxation of G1/G2 preserves the closed-work-observation invariant by ISOLATION (R1: co-opened writer in a .kw leg; parent tree untouched until the G4-held merge) and by CONTRACT (R2: observes:scratch gate reads only .cache/scratch); every R1 fail-closed precondition miss returns today's write_awaits_drain hold byte-identical; and the four chains are green with NO symptom-masked green."

## Disproof Attempt — battery results (all against the REAL worktree CLIs on scratch git repos; zero repo files touched)

1. R1 co-run — NOT REFUTED. Real fixture (g read ∥ w write): tick1 opens g alone (G1 intact); tick2 co-opens w as a size-1 leg group (running set mixed read+leg-write). ISOLATION verified: during the co-run the parent working tree carries zero production dirt, w's bytes exist only under .kw/legs/**, and the only HEAD movement was a commit touching kaola-workflow/test-project/.cache/** (workflow-state band) exactly as claimed.
2. G4 merge fence under co-run — NOT REFUTED and load-bearing. close-node w while g live → refuse merge_awaits_read_drain, ZERO mutation (w in_progress, no scripts/a.js in parent). After g drains, the same close merges cleanly (fence is a hold, not a deadlock) — scripts/a.js lands, w completes.
3. Fail-closed matrix — NOT REFUTED; byte-identity PROVEN against main. Materialized main@866421aa's scripts via git archive and ran identical fixtures on both CLIs. KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0 → write_awaits_drain + parallel_writes_off; live lane_group → lane_group_live; dirty parent → parent_dirty; validator crash on --parallel-safe (sabotaged wrapper) → parallel_safe_indeterminate. Every hold: zero mutation, no leg provisioned, and the JSON envelope byte-identical to main's modulo the single additive serialDegradeReason field. Note: the overlaps_live_writer row is freeze-pre-empted in practice — the freeze wall refuses overlapping AND case-variant sequence antichains, so that arm is defensive-only (validator-drift/tamper), while the crash arm is the runtime-reachable one and held fail-closed.
4. Mixed-set crash-reconcile — NOT REFUTED. state:'opening' + w opening:true → reconcile-running-set rolls forward; both members + lane_group + leg manifest survive.
5. R2a freeze grammar — NOT REFUTED. Real validator: scratch on adversarial-verifier → in-grammar; on code-reviewer / security-reviewer / main-session-gate → observes_scope_role_invalid (handoff --freeze-checked surfaces plan_invalid with the typed token, plan un-mutated); write set → observes_scope_not_read; unknown scope → observes_scope_unsupported. plan_hash differs with/without the annotation — hash-covered.
6. R2b band cap — NOT REFUTED, plus adversarial extras. Over a genuinely dirty parent: scratch-gate ∥ docs/decisions writer co-opens LEGLESS; docs/api.md / CHANGELOG.md / README.md / docs/workflow-state-contract.md writers all HOLD (parent_dirty, zero mutation); un-annotated gate holds even a qualifying writer; annotated gate ∥ production writer holds. Extras: a second PLAIN live read alongside the scratch gate forces the hold (every-live-read rule); the writer's OWN .cache evidence file co-opens while a FOREIGN node's evidence file holds. Traversal/glob/dir-shaped write-set evasions fenced upstream by the freeze wall + the plan_hash integrity prologue.
7. Symptom-masked-green probe — none found. The shipped pins use a REAL git leg-provisioning harness (subprocess CLI, real worktrees), and the merge-fence pin asserts refuse+reason equality. Re-ran the touched suites: test-adaptive-node 1551, test-validation-allowband 32, test-adaptive-handoff 135, walkthrough "simulation passed" — all green.

## n2's two findings — explicit dispositions
- n2-R1 (gate blindness): REAL fence-coverage gap, FENCED at verdict granularity → legitimate non-blocking follow-up (A1). Mechanically reachable; NOT constructible as a verdict-time violation (atomically-closed docs-band work; misbehaving writer refused before merge; no documented path issues open-ready inside the gate window).
- n2-R2 (testConsumedExtra unthreaded): REAL, demonstrated at runtime → follow-up (A2). Fork-only; bounded to a disobedient-gate spurious-refute; one-arg thread-through.

## Verdict
NOT-REFUTED (high confidence). The change survives refutation: R1 preserves closed-work-observation by isolation (leg + G4-held merge), R2 by the scratch contract + band cap; every fail-closed row byte-identical to main; no symptom-masked green. The two confirmed findings are genuine but non-blocking follow-ups (neither is a verdict-time violation; neither weakens the shipped fail-closed matrix).
